sections.title: Enchanting the mirrors
Talking to Asgarnia without the required quests

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia with the required quests

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Howdy stranger. What brings you out to these parts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing much - What are you doing here? It doesn't seem like there's much to do out here in the desert!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well, that's where you'd be wrong. I work for the Archaeological Society of Varrock, and have been excavating around here recently.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Was there something you specifically wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests? Call it a hunch, but you look like the type of man who might...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well, it's funny you should say that. I'm not sure if I would really call it a quest as such, but I found this ancient stone tablet in one of my excavations, and it would really help me out if you could go and take it back to the digsite for me and get it examined.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: It's very old, and I don't recognise any of the inscriptions on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, I was heading that way anyway. Any particular person at the digsite you want me to talk to?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: His name's Terry Balando. Give it to nobody but him. I'm sorry, I can't entrust you with the actual tablet I found, it is far too valuable to give away, but I took these etchings - they should be enough for him to make

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: a preliminary translation on. Come back and let me know what he says, I would hate to waste my time excavating anything that isn't worth my time as a world famous archaeologist!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. Playing delivery boy doesn't really sound much like fun to me.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well, okay. Can't say as I blame you for thinking that. I'll go take it there myself later I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you, anyway?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: You don't recognise me??? I am the world famous Asgarnia Smith, Archaeologist extraordinaire!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: I am the one who discovered the long forgotten Temple of Ikov! The one who unearthed the strange trap filled arena in Brimhaven!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: I'm the leading archaeological expert of our time! I was voted archaeologist of the year four years running by the Varrock Herald! Are you REALLY sure you've never heard of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I really haven't.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well then, I'm confused. Why did you come over and speak to me if you don't know who I am? What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing really. I'm just wandering around for no good reason.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Uh-huh. Well, if you'll excuse me, I have a lot more work to do before I can bed down for the night.

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: So what did Terry Balando say about those etchings? Did he give you a translation for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... yeah... about that... I kind of didn't go and speak to him yet...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well what are you waiting for? A written invitation? All I want you to do is take those etchings up to the digsite for me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll do that then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... yeah... about that... I kind of lost those etchings you gave me...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well how in the world did you do that? (sigh) Okay, I'll make another set, but try not to lose these ones this time!

sections.title: Talking to Terry

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you Terry Balando?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: That's right, who wants to know...?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Ah yes, I recognise you! You're the fellow who found that strange artefact about Zaros for the museum, aren't you? What can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had some etchings to deliver to you, but I don't have them on me...

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Then... you better go get them for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. I was in the desert down by the Bedabin Camp, and I found an archaeologist who asked me to deliver this to you.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: You spoke to the legendary Asgarnia Smith??? Quickly, let me see what he had to give you! He is always at the forefront of archaeological breakthroughs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what does the inscription say? Anything interesting?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: This... this is fascinating! These cuneiforms seem to predate even the settlement we are excavating here... Yes, yes, this is most interesting indeed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you translate it for me?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Well, I am not familiar with this particular language, but the similarities inherent in the pictographs seem to show a prevalent trend towards a syllabary consistent with the phonemes we have discovered in this excavation!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... So, can you translate it for me or not?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Well, unfortunately this is the only example of this particular language I have ever seen, but I might be able to make a rough translation, of sorts...

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: It might be slightly obscure on the finer details, but it should be good enough to understand the rough meaning of what was originally written. Please, just wait a moment, I will write up what I can

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: translate into a journal for you. Then you can take it back to Asgarnia, I think he will be extremely interested in the translation!

sections.title: Talking to Terry after delivering the etchings

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: There you go, that book contains the sum of my translating ability. If you would be so kind as to take that back to Asgarnia, I think it will reassure him that he is on the

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: right track for a find of great archaeological importance

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! You write really quickly don't you?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: What can I say? It's a skill I picked up through my many years of taking field notes!

sections.title: Talking to Terry after receiving the translation

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Hello again. Was that translation any use to Asgarnia?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I, uh, kind of didn't take it to him yet...

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Whyever not? You're the strangest delivery boy I've ever met!

sections.title: Talking to Terry after losing the translation

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that translation you gave me...

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Oh, no matter, I mostly remember what it said anyway! Here you go!

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia after receiving the translation

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: So what did Terry Balando say about those etchings? Did he give you a translation for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, he did. But I don't have it with me.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well could you go and get me that translation? It could be vital!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, he did. I have it here in this book.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Did you take a ready of this? It will do you good to understand how this profession works. Here, have a read.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I did. Kind of boring really.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Excellent. Just give me a moment to read this, and talk to me again in a second.

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia after delivering the translation

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Hmmm. Interesting. It seems to me like there's some kind of treasure hidden out in the desert.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: So what do you say? Fancy being a treasure hunter like me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... don't you mean archaeologist?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Yeeees... An archaeologist...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: It's all this hot sun getting to me I think.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well anyway, let's just assume there is a treasure hidden in the desert somewhere, and let's just say for the sake of argument that maybe if I found a big stash of gold and treasure I wouldn't necessarily just hand it

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: all over to the Museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Let's also say, purely hypothetically, that if there were such a big stash of treasure and someone were to help me find it, then that hypothetical personal might be entitled to, oh, let's say a purely for the sake of

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: argument thirty percent split...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fifty percent.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: That's right!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: A purely for the sake of argument fifty-fifty split on this hypothetical treasure, should it exist...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Do you see where I'm going with this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to help you find some treasure for a fifty percent share, as long as I don't tell anybody, and your reputation as an esteemed archaeologist with the Museum of Varrock remains intact, and nobody

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: discovers you're actually just a treasure hunter?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Uh... yes, but the way you said it makes it sound like I'm doing something wrong...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: So what do you say? Partners?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I guess nobody is really going to lose out on anything, and we don't even know if there is any treasure...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aw, go on then. Count me in.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Good lad! Well, if we split up we'll be able to find treasure quicker.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: I'll continue searching around here in this Bedabin Camp, you head due South to the Bandit Village. If either of us find anything, we'll come find each other and say what, okay?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: You head due South, see what you can find out about this tablet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This all sounds very immoral, and I don't want any part of your seedy little schemes.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Aw, c'mon, cut me a break here man! Look, nobody is really getting hurt, right? This treasure is either going to lie around in some underground ruin, or lie around in some museum for

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: people to look at!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: It makes sense for the people to go to all the risk to get a little payback for it, right? Besides, anything of real historical value we can hand over to the museum - we'll just keep the cash for

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: ourselves!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: C'mon... what do you say? Partners?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't trust anything about your little scheme, including you! I want nothing to do with this!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Eh, you'll come running back when you realize just how much money we could be talking here.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: I'll see you again when you come crawling back, begging to be cut in on the deal.

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia after agreeing to hunt for treasure

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Hello again. You find any signs of that treasure yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well, head south to that bandit camp. I bet if there's anything out here, they'll know about it.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Don't let on it might be valuable though! Those thieves will steal it as soon as look at you!

sections.title: Talking to Terry after delivering his translation

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Hello again. Was that translation any use to Asgarnia?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it was, thanks!

sections.title: Talking to a bandit

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: What do you want lad?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here on an archaeological expedition for the Museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I believe there may be some interesting artefacts in the area.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: You are a crazy man. The only thing you will find out here in the desert is your death.

sections.title: Talking to Eblis before asking the bartender about the diamonds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I represent the Museum of Varrock, and I have reason to believe there may be some kinds of artefacts of historical significance in the nearby area...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Ah yes. The only time people care about our existence is when they think they have something to gain from us.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: I have nothing to say to you. You and your kind are not welcome here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, if I can just have a few minutes of your time to ask some questions...?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: (sigh) I suppose I can spare you that. What do you wish to know about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are all of the people here so hostile? You would think I was asking you for money instead of just for answers to a few questions...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: It is a long story, and I doubt you have much interest in hearing it. Your sort never are, you just take what you can of ours, and then abandon us once more to the desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'd be quite interested to hear what it is you have to say to excuse the attitude everybody in this village seems to have.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Ah, it all begun many generations ago, when our ancestors were the proud rulers of these lands...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: My ancestors lived far to the North of here, and our lands stretched from the sea in the East to the river Lum, and the mountain of ice. From coast to coast, North to South, our domain was absolute.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Our god was kind to us, and blessed us with prosperity and happiness, and in return we were merciless to his enemies wherever we found them.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Then came the betrayal.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Our god was banished, leaving us helpless to our fates.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Without his protection, we were forced to fend for ourselves once more, against the enemies that sought to destroy us through their petty jealousies.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: But we did not succumb without fighting! The spiteful Saradomin and pathetic Zamorak warred with each other, but the hatred they had for each other was as nothing to the hatred they held towards us!

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: With each battle they waged, we lost more and more land, unable to fight on all fronts, and were pushed further and further South into this gods-forsaken desert.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Our greatest hero, Azzanadra, was finally trapped in a strange stone structure to the South of here, and bound within by terrible powers...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: And with that our lands, our homes, our very lives were stolen from us! Too weak to reclaim what was rightfully ours, we made our homes here, knowing that someday Azzanadra will

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: return with his magnificent power, and bring us back to our former glory...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're upset because of something that happened hundreds of years ago?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems to me like maybe you should find some closure, and let the past go...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: The insults heaped upon my race will never be forgotten, will never be forgiven and will never again be overlooked.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Someday, a harsh wind will blow upon this land, uncovering the wrongs of the past, and we will get back what is rightfully ours. Until such a day we will bide our time here, and will

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: always be ready with our blades for our righteous vengeance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't really care what your story is to be honest, there is no excuse for such rudeness or hostility.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have done nothing wrong to you, but everybody here treats me like I have committed some great crime against the village.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: That is because, from our point of view, you have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Just because I entered your village?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: You have no right to be here! You have no right to the life you have, for it was taken at our expense!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whatever... No wonder all you loonies live out here in the desert by yourselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if you knew anything about some treasure somewhere around here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some evidence that there might be some kind of treasure hidden very close to this village...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: If I knew of any treasure I would not choose to spend my life in this gods-forsaken desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about some kind of fortress nearby? I have reason to believe there is, or at least used to be, some kind of fortress very close to here...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Nobody would build anything in this wasteland unless they were forced to, to survive.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: I know of no fortress, I know of no reason why anyone would ever bother doing anything out here in the desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, there was nothing I really wanted to ask you about.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Yes, it is exactly like your sort to waste my time in such a way.

sections.title: Talking to the bartender

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: If you're not buying anything, I have nothing to say to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As I understand it there's some kind of hidden treasure in these parts...

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Look around pal. Does it looks like there's any treasure near here?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: If I were you I'd get lost before someone takes a dislike to your face and removes it for you.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: You ain't got the 650 coins it costs to buy it, and I'm glad 'cos I didn't want to serve you anyway.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: What's that? You wanna buy a beer? It'll cost ya 650 coins.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: There you go. Now get out, we don't like your sort around here.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Get out of my bar then! We don't like your sort round here!

sections.title: Talking to the bartender after buying a drink

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: You've had your drink, now get out of here. I have nothing to say to the-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, Wait! Look, I'm here on an archaeological expedition for the Museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am only here looking for artefacts...

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Oh really? Our inheritance is only sand and death. What do you expect to find out here in this desert forsaken by the gods?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As I understand it there's some kind of hidden treasure in these parts...

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Look around pal. Does it looks like there's any treasure near here?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: If I were you I'd get lost before someone takes a dislike to your face and removes it for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard a rumour about four diamonds or crystals...

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: The four diamonds of Azzanadra??? How came you to know of this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've heard of them then?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: It's just a fairy tale for children. Maybe one of the village elders might know more, but it's not really something I care about.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Now get out of here, your sort isn't welcome in my bar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Certain things have led me to believe there may be some kind of ruined fortress around here...

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Doubt it. What in the world would someone need to guard against in the middle of this desert?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: A bad attack of sand? I think you're on the wrong track, mister so-called archaeologist.

sections.title: Talking to the bartender after asking about the diamonds and before talking to Eblis

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: You've had your drink, now get out of here. I have nothing to say to the-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, Wait! Look, I'm here on an archaeological expedition for the Museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am only here looking for artefacts...

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Oh really? Our inheritance is only sand and death. What do you expect to find out here in this desert forsaken by the gods?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me all you know about the Diamonds of Azzanadra.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Not that I think it's any of your business, but when I was a child I remember hearing the legend.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: I don't recall it particularly well, other than they are said to contain an incredible power.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: If you really want to hear more about it you'd be best off finding someone who cares about the past, and the history of this area, and stop bothering me.

sections.title: Talking to Eblis after asking the bartender about the diamonds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I represent the Museum of Varrock, and I have reason to believe there may be some kinds of artefacts of historical significance in the nearby area...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Ah yes. The only time people care about our existence is when they think they have something to gain from us.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: I have nothing to say to you. You and your kind are not welcome here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, if I can just have a few minutes of your time to ask some questions...?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: (sigh) I suppose I can spare you that. What do you wish to know about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So tell me... Did you ever hear of something called the Diamonds of Azzanadra?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: This is the treasure which you seek???

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Please accept my apologies noble sir! I thought you were but some opportunistic thief, looking to steal what heritage we have left! Now I see that you are in fact a brave adventurer,

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: looking to restore our glories back upon us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah... So anyway, you have heard of them?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Heard of them? Of course I have heard of them! They are the legacy of the great Mahjarrat hero, Azzanadra!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... do you have any idea where they might be? I have a feeling they will be very valuable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, valuable as historical artefacts I mean, obviously.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: They were stolen by warriors of the false god Zamorak generations ago. When you find the warriors, you will find the diamonds.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: I suspect they will not willingly part with such objects of power however.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Beware too, for these warriors are very powerful; they have taken the powers of the diamonds into themselves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Each diamond has an elemental quality...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: There is the Diamond of Blood, the Diamond of Ice, the Diamond of Smoke and the Diamond of Shadow.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: You should expect the warriors to have taken some aspect of these diamonds as their own...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea how I could track down these warriors somehow, then?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: There is an ancient spell I know of that may spy upon such power... But it will require a few ingredients for it to work.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Should you be willing to get these ingredients for me, I will be able to locate the rough area where each of these warriors has taken refuge. The spell is imprecise, but it should help you get on the

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: right track in your search.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Is your desire for our freedom strong enough? Will you gather the ingredients for this spell for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, what do you need?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: For this spell, I will need to make some scrying glasses. I will need enough so that we can view the realm in its entirety.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: When enchanted, the scrying glass will be able to let us view any area that has been influenced by the presence of the Diamonds of Azzanadra.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, but what exactly do you need for this spell?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Well, six scrying glasses should be sufficient. For each scrying glass, I will need two magic logs, a steel bar and some molten glass. This makes a total of 12 magic logs, 6 pieces of molten

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: glass, and 6 steel bars.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: In addition, for the actual spell to enchant the glasses, I will require one set of normal bones, some ash, some charcoal and a single blood rune.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Do you understand me, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quick question; what kind of bones do you need?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Standard bones. Other types of bones are of no use to me in this spell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a slightly odd collection of ingredients, but I shouldn't have too much trouble getting those for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I don't feel like going on a shopping trip for you right now.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: As you wish. I should have known not to get my hopes up that our long cursed life may soon be at an end...

sections.title: Talking to Eblis after agreeing to get ingredients

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some of the stuff you are looking for with me...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Thank you, pass over the ingredients so that I may begin working on the spell.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Before I can complete the spell I will still need the following items;

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: 12 magic logs 6 steel bars 6 molten glass

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: 1 bones, 1 ashes, 1 charcoal and 1 blood rune

sections.title: Giving Eblis ingredients
Magic logs
Steel bar
Molten glass
Ashes

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Thank you, that is enough ash for the spell.

sections.title: Bones

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Thank you, those are enough bones for the spell.

sections.title: Charcoal

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Thank you, that is enough charcoal for the spell.

sections.title: Blood rune

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Thank you, that blood rune should be sufficient for the spell.

sections.title: Talking to Eblis after giving all ingredients

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Excellent! Those are all the ingredients I need to create the scrying glasses.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: I will find a suitable spot in the desert to the East of here, and set them up. When you are ready to begin your search, please come and find me there, I will show you how to utilise the

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: mirrors to find the diamonds.

sections.title: Talking to Eblis again after giving all ingredients

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Meet me again in the desert East of here, I will use these ingredients to create a scrying glass for you.

sections.title: Talking to Eblis at the mirrors

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can you give me any help on where to find these warriors and their diamonds?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: No, the magic used in this spell is powerful, but inaccurate. The direction the scrying glass faces is roughly the direction you will find the warrior, but I'm afraid I

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: can't be any more help than that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand why there are six mirrors when there are only four diamonds...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: As I say, the enchantment is very inaccurate.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: I can only focus upon the aura the diamonds have left behind them, so any place where the Diamonds were present for a significant period of time will still be shown - such as the Bandit Camp where I make my home.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: My apologies, but magic is an inaccurate art in many respects.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Don't forget to come back here when you have collected all four diamonds.

sections.title: Looking at a mirror
Talking to Asgarnia after learning about the Diamonds of Azzanadra

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Hello again. You find any signs of that treasure yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not as such... Although I think I'm onto something of a promising lead...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you ever hear of something called the Diamonds of Azzanadra?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Diamonds of Azzanadra? That sounds very promising indeed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you have heard of them?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Nope, never heard of them before, but you said diamonds! Diamonds are always promising!

sections.title: Shadow diamond
Talking to Rasolo

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Greetings friend. I am Rasolo, the famous merchant. Would you care to see my wares?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, actually I was looking for something specific...

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Hmmmm? And what would that be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am looking for one of the Diamonds of Azzanadra. I have reason to believe it may be somewhere around here...

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Ahhh... The Shadow Diamond...

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: I know the object of which you speak. It is guarded by a fearsome warrior known as Damis, they say, who lives in the shadows, invisible to prying eyes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I find this 'Damis' then?

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Well now... perhaps we can help each other out here.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: I have in my possession a small trinket, a ring, that allows its wearer to see the unseen...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much do you want for it?

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Ah, but it is not for sale... As such...

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: I am offering to trade it for an item that was rightfully mine, but that was stolen by a bandit named Laheeb.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: The item is question is a gilded cross, that has some sentimental value to myself. I wish for you to recover this item for me, and I will happily let you have my ring of visibility.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this Laheeb?

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Well, as a travelling merchant I have roamed these lands for many years...

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: To the far east of here there is an area that is dry and barren like the desert... it is called...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: ...Yes, Al Kharid. Now to the south of Al Kharid there is a passageway, it is called the...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shantay Pass.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: ...Yes. I didn't realise you had travelled there yourself.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Anyway, when you have gone through the Shantay Pass, you will find yourself in a hostile desert... You will need to bring water with you to keep your life. Now, to the south-west of this pass, you will find a small

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: village...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Bedabin camp.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: If you know where Laheeb lives, why did you ask me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't. Sorry, please continue.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Well, okay then. Anyway, south of this encampment there is an area where few have ever been... It is a village of murderous bandits, and treacherous

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: thieves... This is where Laheeb makes his home.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: He will have hidden his stolen treasure somewhere in that village, I am sure of it. When you find his loot, you will find my gilded cross. Return it to me, and I will reward you with my ring

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: of visibility, so that you may find Damis. Does this seem fair to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a problem. I'll be back with your cross before you know it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like too much effort to me. I'll find this Damis by myself.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: As you wish.

sections.title: Talking to Rasolo after declining to help him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this Damis again?

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: I have already told you, he is of the shadows. You will see neither him nor his lair without the aid of magic.

sections.title: Talking to Rasolo after agreeing to help him

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Have you retrieved my gilded cross for me yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet...

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Well what seems to be the problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this Laheeb?

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Well, as a travelling merchant I have roamed these lands for many years...

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: To the far east of here there is an area that is dry and barren like the desert... it is called...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: ...Yes, Al Kharid. Now to the south of Al Kharid there is a passageway, it is called the...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shantay Pass.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: ...Yes. I didn't realise you had travelled there yourself.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Anyway, when you have gone through the Shantay Pass, you will find yourself in a hostile desert... You will need to bring water with you to keep your life. Now, to the south-west of this pass, you will find a small

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: village...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Bedabin camp.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: If you know where Laheeb lives, why did you ask me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't. Sorry, please continue.

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: Well, okay then. Anyway, south of this encampment there is an area where few have ever been... It is a village of murderous bandits, and treacherous

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: thieves... This is where Laheeb makes his home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't I just buy your ring?

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: No, it is not for sale. Some things are more important than money, and the return of my gilded cross is one of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is Damis near here, then?

lines.speaker: Rasolo
lines.text: You would be surprised to know just how close he is...

sections.title: Attempting to open Laheeb's chest without a lockpick
Blood diamond
Entering the Hair of the Dog tavern

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: M-my lord Malak, what brings you here?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Hmmm? You know very well that your tithe is due, Roavar.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: I am here to collect it this time.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Y-yes of course, sire, but surely your graciousness is too important to perform such menial duties such as this himself?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Is this not Gadderanks job, your most high beneficence?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Indeed it is Roavar, but I cannot find that incompetent fool. And assuming you are not the one who wishes to tell Lord Drakan that his tithe has not been collected, I saw

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: fit to take on the duty myself this time.

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Of course my lord, I meant no disrespect.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: So you have the tithe ready for me?

lines.speaker: Roavar
lines.text: Of course my lord, I trust you will find it all there and in good order.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Hmmm. Well, for your sake you had better hope that indeed it is.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: The mood I am in for having to come to this wolfen cesspit means any shortfall is coming from you directly.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Then again... perhaps this was not a total waste of my time after all.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: You there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: M-me?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Yes, you. Your bearing suggests you are not one of the usual peasants, and your stench suggests you are not one of these filthy wolfen folk.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Come here, I would like to know what you are doing in these lands.

sections.title: Talking to Malak

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: A human, eh? Don't think that silly little ring of yours will fool me. Give me one good reason why I should not just take you to Lord Drakan now.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: A human, eh? Give me one good reason why I should not just take you to Lord Drakan now.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: You had better make it a good one too, or you will not survive the night, I'll wager.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am here to worship the almighty Zamorak! Yay! Go Zamorak!

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: I see. You are a moron. You have probably settled right in with the rest of the idiots in this pathetic excuse for a village.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Unfortunately for you, that is not a good enough reason to explain your presence here.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Now tell me the reason for your coming here, or I will ensure you suffer a horrible fate indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am here only to serve the mighty Drakan. Yup, Drakan, he's the man.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: I see. I would perhaps be more inclined to believe you if I could not smell the death blood of his brother Draynor upon you.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: What are you real intentions here?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Really? That is interesting, that you would want to give your life by tresspassing in this land for such an unbelievable reason.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Now speak, what is your purpose in coming here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I came here looking for you, oh mighty Malak, so that I might serve your glory.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Please. Do not think that I am so vain and foolish as to allow you to avoid my question with such obvious sycophancy.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Now tell me the reason behind your being here, or I will ensure that you suffer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am here to kill Lord Drakan, and every stinking one of his vampyre brood!

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Hah! Most entertaining, human!

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Now tell me the reason you are here, or we shall soon see who will be killing whom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am here looking for a special diamond... I have reason to believe it is somewhere in this vicinity, and it is probably in the possession of a warrior of Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fairly sure it will have some kind of magical aura or something too. I don't suppose you've seen it, or know where it might be?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Interesting... Well perhaps we can come to a little... arrangement, human.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: I may have information that may assist you, but you in turn will have to do something for me. What do you say? Do you think we could come to some form of

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: agreement?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, what kind of something? No offence, but you're not exactly the trustworthy type...

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Ah, you have a healthy sense of paranoia, I see. It is not a particularly unfair request on my part...

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: All I ask is that you ensure that the current owner of the diamond is killed. For my part, I will let you know his whereabouts, and how exactly to kill him.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: When he is dead, you may take the diamond from his corpse and do with it what you will. I have no interest in such baubles.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: So what say you? A life for a diamond. As a mark of good faith, I will give you some information free:

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: The current owner of this diamond is named Dessous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I can't see any drawback. Okay, I accept your offer. Now tell me what you know.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: What I know? Hah! After you have been alive for as long as I, the things I know are enough to fill a library.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: I'm afraid you will need to be a little more specific.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't see exactly how this bargain benefits you... Why exactly do you want Dessous killed anyway?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: That is an impertinent question to demand from myself.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: However, if it will help seal our deal, I will let you know some of the details.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: As you may or may not know, myself and my blood kin are the rulers of this land, all serving under Lord Drakan. However, we tend not to die of old age or similar

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: natural causes, which means that to gain another Lords tithe or land, there often need to be...

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Unnatural causes of death involved. Let us just say, that Dessous is in control of some land that I myself would like some say in, and that it is not in my interest to be seen to be responsible for the

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: death of a fellow Lord.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: It would however be extremely advantageous to myself should some random human adventurer take it upon themself to remove this rival for me... Do you understand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... I think so...

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Good, we understand each other then. We will both benefit from the death of Dessous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this Dessous?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: He currently resides in a graveyard to the South-East of here. You will not be able to move the gravestone which he lies beneath however, you will need to find some way to

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: lure him out from his tomb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how exactly would I go about doing that?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Well, he is a vampyre, so fresh blood would almost certainly entice him out.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: However, even though he is a frail and decrepit example of our species, he will be able to kill a weakling human such as yourself extremely easily. Having him in a bloodlust as he does so, will not make

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: your job any easier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what advice can you give me on killing Dessous?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: As ancient and weak as Dessous is, he is still more than a match for the likes of you.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: That is, assuming you were to fight him fairly.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: My proposal would be for you to even the odds up a little bit...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would I go about doing that?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Well, my plan would be as follows. First, take a silver bar to the man living in the sewers in Draynor. He was an assistant to Count Draynor in some of his...

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: more interesting experiments many years past. Tell him you need a sacrificial offering pot. He will know what you speak of, it is a unique type of container used in various ancient vampyric ceremonies.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Then take the pot to Entrana and get it blessed by the Head Monk. This will lend the pot some holy power.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: If you then bring that silver pot back to me, I will provide you with some fresh blood, to put into it. To that pot of blood, you will add some crushed garlic, and some spice to disguise the smell.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Use that pot of blood upon Dessous' tomb, and he will be unable to resist rising and drinking from it.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: The combination of garlic, silver, and blessings from Saradomin will act upon him as a poison, and allow you to kill him.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: This is just my suggestion of course, you may ignore it if you wish, although I offer no guarantees of your ability to defeat him otherwise.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Was there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind, I will figure out all I need to know by myself.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: As you wish. Come and see me when you have managed to kill Dessous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't trust you, or anything you say. I'm afraid I have to decline.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: As you wish human. I doubt anybody near here knows of the diamond you seek, however.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: If you wish to claim it as your own, you have little choice but to accept my bargain. I will wait here until such time as you change your mind.

sections.title: Talking to Malak after agreeing to kill Dessous

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Why are you still here? I notice Dessous still lives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this Dessous?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: He currently resides in a graveyard to the South-East of here. You will not be able to move the gravestone which he lies beneath however, you will need to find some way to

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: lure him out from his tomb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how exactly would I go about doing that?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Come and see my when you have prepared a silver ritual pot in the manner I have told you.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: I will ensure that you get some fresh blood that you may taint with garlic and spices, to lure our Dessous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I supposed to kill Dessous again?

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Take a silver bar to the man in the Draynor sewers. He will fashion a ritualistic pot for you, which you should then take to Entrana and get blessed.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: When you have done that, come back here and speak to me, I will provide you with some fresh blood which you will then crush some garlic into, and then add some spices to hide the garlic.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: In this way, you will be able to lure him from his tomb and he should be sufficiently weakened to be vulnerable to your attacks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind, I will figure out all I need to know by myself.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: As you wish. Come and see me when you have managed to kill Dessous.

sections.title: Talking to Ruantun

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Ruantun
lines.text: You sshould not be down here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you an assistant to Count Draynor?

lines.speaker: Ruantun
lines.text: I usssed to have that honour... Why do you ssseek me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a silver bar with me, I was wondering if you could make it into a 'sacrificial offering pot' for me?

lines.speaker: Ruantun
lines.text: Yesss, of courssse... There you are, put it to good usssse...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand that you can make me a 'sacrificial offering pot' if I bring you a bar of silver?

lines.speaker: Ruantun
lines.text: And where did you hear thisss?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was from Malak in Canifis.

lines.speaker: Ruantun
lines.text: Ah, I sssee... Yesss, I know how to make sssuch an item... It has been many yearsss sssince I have needed to however...

lines.speaker: Ruantun
lines.text: It is not my wisssh to quessstion your desssire for sssuch an item. I wasss merely sssurprisssed that one sssuch as you would make sssuch a requessst...

lines.speaker: Ruantun
lines.text: I will happily make you thisss pot, but you mussst bring me a bar of sssilver... Alasss, I can no longer collect my own ingredientsss, and mussst remain here...

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Many greetings. Welcome to our fair island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I was wondering, can you quickly bless this for me?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: A somewhat strange request, but I see no harm in it. There you go. May Saradomin walk with you.

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest with a silver pot of your own blood

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I was wondering, can you quickly bless this for me?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: A somewhat strange request, but I see no harm in... Wait a moment... is that BLOOD?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... yes...

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Why do you want me to bless a pot full of someones blood???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, don't worry, it's not 'someones', it's my own!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: I... see... Well it is an unusual thing, but I see no harm in it. Take this, and walk in peace with Saradomin.

sections.title: Talking to Malak with the blessed pot

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found Ruantun in Draynors sewers. He made me this pot, now where can I get some fresh blood to fill it with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: There you go. As fresh as it gets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for nothing.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Come and speak to me again when you have managed to kill Dessous.

sections.title: Adding garlic to the blessed pot
Adding spices to the blessed pot
Using the blessed pot on Dessous's tomb
When Dessous uses his bat attack

lines.speaker: Dessous
lines.text: Hssssssssssss

sections.title: After defeating Dessous

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's Dessous dead, but where is the Diamond he was supposed to have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If Malak lied to me about it, he is going to pay!

sections.title: Talking to Malak after defeating Dessous

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Ah, the wandering hero returns! I take it you have dispatched poor old Dessous for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quit playing games with me, Malak. I want that diamond, and I want it now!

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Do not take that tone of voice with me, meat. You should be thankful I have allowed you your life.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Here, take your precious little bauble.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: I will take that silver pot from you as well, humans are not meant to possess such artefacts. Now get out of my sight, our deal is complete, and if I see you here again I will not hesistate to take you to

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Lord Drakan.

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: He will be extremely pleased to meet the murderer of poor Dessous, I suspect.

sections.title: Talking to Malak after obtaining the blood diamond

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Be lucky I have let you live, meat. Our deal is done, I wish no further dealing with you.

sections.title: Talking to Malak after losing the blood diamond

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the Diamond of Blood? I know you have it!

lines.speaker: Malak
lines.text: Do not take that tone of voice with me, meat. Here, take your bauble. I have no use for it.

sections.title: Ice diamond
Talking to the troll child

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: Waaaaaaa!

sections.title: Giving the troll child an item other than sweet food

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: WAAAAAAAA!

sections.title: Giving the troll child sweet food

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there little troll... Take this and dry those tears...

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: (sniff)

sections.title: Talking to the troll child after giving them sweet food

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: -sniff- H-hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why so sad, little troll?

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: It was the bad man! He hurt my mommy and daddy! He made them all freezey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bad man...?

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: He said it was because they stole his diamond! But they never did! They found it, and didn't know who it belonged to!

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: My mommy always told me stealing is wrong, they would never steal from someone!

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: Then he did some wavey hand thing and my mommy and daddy got frozified!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A diamond you say? Listen, I think I might be able to help your parents, but I need that Diamond in return.

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: -sniff- I don't think they really wanted it anyway, they would have given it back to the bad man if he'd asked before freezifying them...

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: I give you my promise mister that if you unfreeze my mommy and daddy, you can have the stupid diamond.

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: Do we have a deal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely. Don't worry kid, I'll get your parents back to you safe and sound.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I can't make any promises about that, and I don't think I have the time to waste trying to defrost some stupid ice trolls.

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: Waaaaaaa!

sections.title: Entering the ice gate for the first time

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. My mommy says that we ice trolls are so cold we can freeze the air around us when we are upset or angry.

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: I'm afraid I must have frozen those gates too much when I was crying, but since you told me you are going to help rescue my mommy and daddy you should be able to squeeze through the bars.

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: You are much smaller than most trolls, it should be easy for you!

sections.title: Entering the ice gate
Talking to the ice troll past the gate

lines.speaker: Ice troll
lines.text: Hur hur hur! Well look here, a puny fleshy human! You should beware of the icy wind that runs through this valley, it will bring a fleshy like you to a cold end indeed!

sections.title: Killing an ice troll
When Kamil uses his freezing spell

lines.speaker: Kamil
lines.text: Sallamakar Ro!

sections.title: After defeating Kamil

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that must have been the 'bad man' that the troll kid was on about... His parents must be up ahead somewhere.

sections.title: Passing through the ice gate after the ice ledge
Talking to a frozen troll
Talking to the troll father before freeing the troll mother

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: Oh thank you! It was really cold in there! But please, you must free my wife as well! Our son is depending on us!

sections.title: Talking to the troll mother before freeing the troll father

lines.speaker: Troll mother
lines.text: Wow, thanks for breaking me out of that ice! But please, my husband is still trapped in there!

sections.title: Talking to the troll parents after freeing them

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: Phew! Am I glad to be out of that big ice cube! Are you okay too darling?

lines.speaker: Troll mother
lines.text: Yes, I thought we were done for! Why ever did that nasty Kamil freeze us up there anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He must have been trying to protect his Diamond...

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: You mean that diamond I found the other day belonged to him? But why didn't he just ask for it back? It's not like I really want it or anything!

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: And how did you know we had that diamond anyway, fleshy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your son told me. That's why I rescued you, it is very important that I have that diamond...

lines.speaker: Troll mother
lines.text: Ooohhhhh, my poor baby! He must have been so worried about us...

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: Yes, but he certainly inherited his Dad's smarts!

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: If he'd told this fleshy that he had the Diamond and not us, we might never have been rescued!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... what? That stupid little troll kid had the diamond all along?

lines.speaker: Troll mother
lines.text: Don't you talk about my baby like that!

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: Now, now dear, all's well that ends well. We've been freed and this fleshy has certainly earned himself that diamond.

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: Let's get out of this terrible place and see our son!

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: Mommy! Daddy! You're free!

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: That's right son, and it's all thanks to this brave adventurer here. Now, make sure you hand over that diamond he was looking for.

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: It has been nothing but trouble for us, let's just get back to our cave and have dinner.

lines.speaker: Troll mother
lines.text: That's right son, it's your favorite tonight too! A big plate of raw mackerel!

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: RAW MACKEREL! YUMMY!

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: Here ya go, [mister/lady]! Thanks for getting my mom and dad away from the bad man!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry about it, just as long as I don't have to go back into that blizzard.

sections.title: Talking to the troll father at the ice gate

lines.speaker: Troll father
lines.text: Thanks again for freeing me from that ice block! I might be a troll, but it was real uncomfortable in there!

sections.title: Talking to the troll mother at the ice gate

lines.speaker: Troll mother
lines.text: Thanks again for freeing me from that ice block! I don't know what my little snookums would have done without us!

sections.title: Talking to the troll child after receiving the ice diamond

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: Thanks for all your help! I'm surprised you managed to survive the blizzard, being a thin skinned fleshy and all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I say? I'm a lot tougher than I look.

sections.title: Talking to the troll child after losing the ice diamond

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that diamond of Ice you gave me...

lines.speaker: Troll child
lines.text: That's okay, it blew back on an icy wind... It's almost like it wants to stay here! Here, take it back, you've earned it.

sections.title: The Ancient Pyramid
Talking to Eblis with one diamond

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have collected one Diamond so far!

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: That is a good start... but Azzanadra is still imprisoned!

sections.title: Talking to Eblis with two diamonds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have collected 2 Diamonds so far!

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: That is a good start... but Azzanadra is still imprisoned!

sections.title: Talking to Eblis with three diamonds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have collected 3 Diamonds so far!

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: That is a good start... but Azzanadra is still imprisoned!

sections.title: Talking to Eblis with four diamonds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I have all four of these Diamonds of Azzanadra, now what?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Azzanadra was our greatest ever hero. He was unkillable, and the cowardly traitors who stole our lands did not know what to do with him, for his hatred for them was as strong as his magics.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: In the end, they cast a spell upon him, to trap him in the stone structure to the South of here.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: They stole his very life force, the essence of his power, and trapped it within four crystals - the very same Four Diamonds which you have now recovered from the brigands who stole from us.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: The four pillars surrounding the structure are keeping the containment spell intact. By placing a diamond into each, you will breach the magical defenses and begin to restore Azzanadra's

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: power, and be able to enter the structure.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Go, place the diamonds, and free my lord Azzanadra!

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: The path will be hard, for his prison is full of traps and danger to prevent his rescue, but he will reward you beyond your wildest dreams when freed!

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Quickly... After all these centuries, Lord Azzanadra is nearly free! You must spare no time, place the Diamonds upon the pillars and enter the pyramid so that you may free him!

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia with all the diamonds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Hello again. How's the treasure hunting going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I've found four diamonds.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: What? But you've been gone ages!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: C'mon, you can be square with me, you must have found more treasure than just four measly diamonds by now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I really haven't.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Hmmm. Well, okay a deal is a deal. Hand two of them over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I think I'm going to need them to gain access to the real treasure...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: REALLY? Great, I love treasure!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Don't worry about two measly diamonds if you think they'll lead to something better!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: I don't usually get out of bed for less than a hundred gold certs!

sections.title: Attempting to replace the diamonds without level 50 Magic
After replacing the diamonds, before going inside
Eblis

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I used the Four Diamonds on the pillars surrounding the pyramid...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Yes? And what happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... they changed colour.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Ah, that was the power being re-absorbed back into them. That should have been sufficient to break the magic seal preventing anyone exiting or entering the structure.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Quickly, you must let Lord Azzanadra know that he is free again after these many years!

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Please, when you have freed Azzanadra, let me know what words he has for his faithful followers...

sections.title: Asgarnia Smith

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Hello again, apprentice archaeologist. How's the treasure hunting going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I found four diamonds...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Great! Pass me my share, and it's a good job all around!

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Um... sorry, I can't.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Huh? You're not trying to break a deal here are ya???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it's just... There were these weird pillars around this pyramid...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They kind of... ate the diamonds...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Pyramid columns ate your diamonds?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: That's a crazy story kid. Why would you go putting diamonds into them in the first place anyway? Diamonds!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Of all things!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this feeling there is some better treasure inside that pyramid, and I needed to put the diamonds in to gain entrance.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well what are you wasting time talking to me for?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Go get me some treasure, pal!

sections.title: Talking to Azzanadra

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: I knew they could not trap me here for long! Well done, friend. Tell me, how goes the war?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: War?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: You do not know of the war for our survival? More time must have passed than I had thought...

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Tell me, what news of great Paddewwa? Do the shining spires of Lassar still stand? And what of glorious Annakarl? The fortress is still intact?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... sorry, I've never heard of them...

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: No! My lord... what has become of you? I cannot hear your voice anymore!

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: My thanks to you friend for your help in freeing me from this accursed place, but it seems I have much to do to make amends. If our empire no longer stands, then it means that we must have failed my lord.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: How long have I been trapped here? Master... have you truly been dispatched from this world?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: My friend, for your efforts in freeing me, I offer you the gift of knowledge. We Mahjarrat were once granted powerful ancient magicks by our lord. I now bestow their power upon you.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: They will replace the knowledge you previously had, but you may switch between them by praying at the altar in this room at any time. May you use them well.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: I trust that we shall meet again, but now I must leave you. Feel free to use the portal I shall create to return here easily in the future!

sections.title: Post-quest
Eblis
After buying his staff

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Greetings. I await the return of my Lord Azzanadra and of our god. I do not know why, but I feel this spot has some significance...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: So have you spoken to my Lord Azzanadra yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: And what words did he have for his followers?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... He didn't really mention you at all, but he did teach me some cool new magic spells.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: It is understandable perhaps... His poor mind must be addled after all of those years of confinement, he would not willingly ignore his followers...

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Anyway, if he has taught you our ancient magics, you may be interested in purchasing an ancient heirloom that was passed down to me. My ancestor fought in the ancient battles using the

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: magic of our god. This heirloom will help you with the speed of your spell-casting.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Normally I could not bear to part with such a priceless relic, but for your help in freeing my Lord Azzanadra, I will be prepared to sell it to you for a mere 80,000 gold.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Are you interested?

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Take care of it, it is the only heirloom from those times I possess, although rumour has it many of our ancient warriors were buried with identical weapons so that they could continue to fight for my Lord in their deaths.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Stranger... Do not abuse my generosity... I cannot possibly sell it for less than 80,000 gold.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: Come back and see me when you have the necessary funds to buy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Eblis
lines.text: As you wish. Bear my offer in mind should you ever change your decision, I will remain here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave you to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got any symbols of your god?
